decision: HOLD - did not publish. No browser action taken.
primary_reason: Today is Saturday 2026-06-06 = Shabbat. The community-agent posting calendar rule is absolute: 'Never post: Friday, Saturday (Shabbat), Jewish holidays.' The Israeli Facebook group is a Hebrew-speaking Israeli audience, so a Shabbat post is both rule-violating and culturally inappropriate.
secondary_reasons: No calendar entry exists for 2026-06-06. The calendar runs Sunday-Thursday only; the next scheduled IL post is 2026-06-08 (Sunday), POST 4 (post_04_tax_guide).

campaign_state.json open blocker (severity MEDIUM): 'Future Hebrew community posts need Brand Guard review. Community posts 3-50 are held as drafts because they were authored before the current Israeli currency, CTA, legal ownership, and register rules.' The next-in-sequence post (POST 4) sits inside this held 3-50 range, so the fallback 'post next unpublished in sequence' is itself blocked.

Unresolved currency contradiction: CLAUDE.md mandates shekels-only for Israeli content (Villa D 1,535,000 ILS, Villa C 1,650,000 ILS, reservation 9,999 ILS), while campaign_state.json sets pricing_rule 'php_only_all_markets' with Israeli prices in PHP. Publishing into this conflict risks a brand-rule violation. Needs human/Claude Code resolution before any priced IL post.
next_action: Resume at the next valid Israeli posting slot, Sunday 2026-06-08, with POST 4 - but only after (a) Brand Guard review clears posts 3-50 and (b) the shekel-vs-PHP currency rule conflict is resolved. If still unresolved by Jun 8, hold again and report.
notes: Cross-checked against the same-day PH run (_completed/2026-06-06/scheduled_ph_post.json), which DID publish. That is correct and consistent: the PH/English audience does not observe Shabbat, so the no-post-Saturday rule applies only to the Israeli market.

Per task instructions, stopped and reported rather than guessing. No Chrome connection was attempted because the decision not to post is determined by rules alone, independent of platform availability.
